reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based solely on prepare_release.sh, which is a developer tool not executed during makepkg. The PKGBUILD uses pinned sources with verified checksums and contains no malicious build/install logic.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model incorrectly flagged prepare_release.sh as a supply-chain risk despite it not being executed during package build/installation
coverage_issues: qwen model reviewed only 5 additional files vs deepseek's 10, potentially missing critical files
Neither model reviewed build/check hooks that might execute during makepkg
false_positives: prepare_release.sh (qwen): Developer script not called by PKGBUILD; go install is for maintainer tooling
learnings: Improve context awareness: Models should better distinguish between build-time scripts vs developer tools
Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD functions (build/prepare/package) over unrelated scripts
Enforce review of all executable hooks and scripts called during makepkg lifecycle
Add explicit checks for network access during build/install phases
